summary: The CI job completed building CPython on Windows x64 successfully (with only non-fatal C4244 warnings), but the test suite failed because test_tcl reported 2 failures. The failure was confirmed as non-flaky: test_tcl failed on the initial run and again on re-run in verbose mode.
root_cause: test_tcl failed with 2 test assertion failures on Windows x64 (switch-case build). The failures persisted on re-run, indicating a deterministic regression rather than a transient issue.

evidence.reason: Explicit failure report from the test runner showing test_tcl produced 2 failures on first run.

evidence.quote: Re-running 1 failed tests in verbose mode in subprocesses
evidence.reason: The test harness re-ran the failing test to check for flakiness.
test_tcl failed again on re-run, confirming the failure is deterministic and not a transient flake.
The test harness explicitly marks the failure as persistent after re-run.
Final summary confirms the job result is FAILURE with both the initial and re-run producing failures.
The test_tcl module reported exactly 2 assertion/test failures and 3 skipped tests.

suggested_fix: Investigate the 2 failing tests within test.test_tcl.TclTest on Windows x64. Run the test locally with verbose output (`python -m test test_tcl -v`) to see which specific test methods failed and the assertion details, then check recent changes to Modules/_tkinter.c or Lib/tkinter/__ related code that could affect Tcl integration on Windows.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
